# Go Green Scrap Pros — Website

Static HTML website for [gogreenscrappros.com](https://gogreenscrappros.com).

## Setup

Run once after cloning:

```bash
npm install
```

This installs `clean-css-cli`, which the pre-commit hook uses to minify CSS.

## CSS Workflow

**Never edit `assets/style.css` directly** — it is auto-generated and will be overwritten.

Edit `assets/source-style.css` instead. The pre-commit hook minifies it into `assets/style.css` automatically on every `git commit`.

To minify manually without committing:

```bash
npm run minify-css
```

## Page Generation

City + service pages (e.g. `mattress-removal-arvada/`) are generated by `generate_pages.py`:

```bash
python3 generate_pages.py
```

This produces 9 services × 7 cities = 63 pages, renders the pricing-templated
static pages (see Pricing below), and refreshes `sitemap.xml`. After running,
commit the output normally — the pre-commit hook will handle CSS minification.

## Pricing

**Never hand-edit a dollar amount directly in a page.** Every published price
on the site comes from `pricing.json` — that's the single place to change a
price.

- `generate_pages.py`'s own service copy (scrap metal, TV recycling) reads
  `pricing.json` directly at generation time.
- Hand-authored pages that mention a price (homepage, contact, the service
  hub pages, `llms.txt`) live as *templates* under `templates/` with
  `{{PRICE_*}}` placeholders instead of literal dollar amounts. Running
  `python3 generate_pages.py` renders each template into its real output
  path (e.g. `templates/mattress-removal__index.html` →
  `mattress-removal/index.html`), substituting every placeholder from
  `pricing.json`.
- To change a price: edit `pricing.json`, run `python3 generate_pages.py`,
  and commit both the JSON change and the regenerated output.
- To edit the *copy* around a price on one of the templated pages, edit the
  file under `templates/`, not the rendered page in the repo root — the
  rendered page gets overwritten on the next build.

## Sitemap

`sitemap.xml` is auto-generated by `generate_pages.py` on every run by
walking the filesystem for pages — deleted pages drop out automatically,
new pages are picked up with no manual step needed.
